info.title: Ishraf Platform Backend API
info.description: Full OpenAPI coverage for all live backend endpoints. /health and /health/ready are documented against the service root, while the rest use /api/v1.

tags.name: Health
tags.description: Service health and readiness endpoints mounted outside /api/v1.

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: Authentication, session lifecycle, password recovery, and current-user endpoints.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: Admin-only user management endpoints for all roles.

tags.name: Academic Structure
tags.description: Admin-only academic years, semesters, grade levels, classes, subjects, and staff assignment endpoints.

tags.name: Students
tags.description: Admin-only student lifecycle, parent linking, and promotion endpoints.

tags.name: Attendance
tags.description: Session-based attendance endpoints used by admin, teachers, and supervisors.

tags.name: Assessments
tags.description: Assessment types, assessments, and student score roster endpoints.

tags.name: Behavior
tags.description: Behavior categories, behavior records, and student behavior timelines.

tags.name: Transport
tags.description: Transport static data, assignments, trips, live locations, and trip student events.

tags.name: Communication
tags.description: Direct messages, announcements, and notification center endpoints.

tags.name: Homework
tags.description: Homework management, submission rosters, and student homework surfaces.

tags.name: Reporting
tags.description: Role dashboards, student-scoped reports, transport summaries, and parent live-status endpoints.

CreateUserRequest.description: Discriminated by role. profile is role-specific: parent(address, relationType), teacher(specialization, qualification, hireDate), supervisor(department), driver(licenseNumber, driverStatus), admin(null or omitted).

get.description:
Return liveness information for the deployed service.

Public endpoint.

Return readiness information after checking database connectivity.

Public endpoint.

post.description:
Change the current authenticated user password.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Request a password reset token for an active account. Staging usually does not expose resetToken in the response.

Public endpoint.

Authenticate with email or phone and issue access and refresh tokens.

Public endpoint.

Revoke the provided refresh token.

Public endpoint.

get.description:
Return the authenticated active user profile used for frontend bootstrap.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Exchange a valid refresh token for a fresh access/refresh pair.

Public endpoint.

Reset the password using a valid reset token. Successful reset revokes existing refresh tokens.

Public endpoint.

get.description:
Return the active academic year and active semester used by operational admin surfaces.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] This is the canonical global context for daily admin operations.

Note: If either the active academic year or active semester is missing, operational endpoints may return 409 Academic context not configured.

Atomically set the active academic year and active semester for operational surfaces.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Use this as the canonical activation flow instead of activating the academic year and semester separately.

Note: The selected semester must belong to the selected academic year.

get.description:
List supervisor assignments with optional academic year, class, and supervisor filters.

Allowed roles: admin.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: supervisorId
parameters.description: Filter by supervisor identifier. Accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

post.description:
Assign a supervisor to a class for an academic year. supervisorId accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Frontend should send the supervisor user id returned by GET /users?role=supervisor. The backend resolves it internally to the stored supervisor profile id.

Note: Legacy integrations may still send the supervisor profile id directly.

Note: If the same value matches a supervisor user id and a different supervisor profile id, the backend rejects the request with SUPERVISOR_ID_AMBIGUOUS instead of guessing.

patch.description:

Note: [NEW] Supervisor assignment updates preserve the existing compatibility and supervisor-resolution rules.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Frontend should send the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher. The backend resolves it internally to the stored teacher profile id.

Note: Legacy integrations may still send the teacher profile id directly.

Note: If the same value matches a teacher user id and a different teacher profile id, the backend rejects the request with TEACHER_ID_AMBIGUOUS instead of guessing.

Link an existing parent to a student. parentId may be either the parent user id returned by /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The backend resolves parentId to the stored parent profile id automatically. Prefer sending the user id returned by /users?role=parent in admin frontend flows.

post.description:
Promote a student to another class for a target academic year while writing structured enrollment state and promotion history.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The promotion writes the target academic enrollment even when the target year is not active yet.

Note: students.class_id is only synchronized immediately when the target year is the active academic year.

patch.description:
Mark one linked parent as the primary parent for that student. parentId may be either the parent user id returned by /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: The backend resolves parentId to the stored parent profile id automatically. Prefer sending the user id returned by /users?role=parent in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: studentId
parameters.description: Student numeric string identifier. This is the same id used by /students/:id.

parameters.name: parentId
parameters.description: Parent identifier. These student-parent endpoints accept either the parent user id from /users?role=parent or the underlying parent profile id.

Update one attendance record status or notes.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Side effects: If the record changes into absent, the internal automation service may create notifications for linked parents.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List attendance sessions for the active academic context with pagination. There is no root /attendance endpoint.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: Empty collections return 200 with items=[] and pagination metadata.

Note: This operational list is scoped to the active academic year and active semester.

Note: teacherId filter accepts the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id. Prefer the user id in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: teacherId
parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

post.description:
Create an attendance session inside the active academic context. Admin teacherId accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: The selected subjectId must have an active subject offering for the resolved semester.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId as the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher.

Side effects: Any newly absent record can trigger parent notifications through the internal automation service.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: The selected subjectId must have an active subject offering for the resolved semester.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId as the teacher user id returned by GET /users?role=teacher.

Create a behavior category definition.

Allowed roles: admin.

get.description:
List behavior records for the active academic context with pagination and filters.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: This operational list is scoped to the active academic year and active semester.

Note: teacherId and supervisorId filters accept the corresponding user ids returned by GET /users or the legacy profile ids. Prefer user ids in admin frontend flows.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: teacherId
parameters.description: Filter by teacher identifier. Accepts the teacher user id from /users?role=teacher or the legacy teacher profile id.

parameters.name: supervisorId
parameters.description: Filter by supervisor identifier. Accepts the supervisor user id from /users?role=supervisor or the legacy supervisor profile id.

post.description:
Create a behavior record inside the active academic context. teacherId and supervisorId accept the matching user ids from /users or the legacy profile ids.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: academicYearId and semesterId may be omitted; the backend resolves the active academic context automatically.

Note: If academicYearId or semesterId is sent, it must match the active context.

Note: Admin frontend flows should send teacherId or supervisorId as the selected account user id from GET /users?role=teacher|supervisor.

Note: Legacy integrations may still send teacher/supervisor profile ids directly.

Side effects: Negative behavior categories trigger parent notifications through the internal automation service.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return one behavior record.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

Update a behavior record payload.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return the student behavior timeline used beside behavior summaries. This endpoint is not paginated.

Allowed roles: admin, teacher, supervisor.

Note: If the student exists but has no behavior records yet, the response remains 200 with zero-safe summary values and records=[].

Derived data: The response combines behavior detail and summary SQL views such as vw_behavior_details and vw_student_behavior_summary.

example.message: Get Student Behavior Records completed successfully

Create or update the current student home location reference. v1 uses admin as the submitting source.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Admin may save pending, approved, or rejected locations. Only approved locations appear in the driver roster.

get.description:
List trips with pagination. Drivers only see trips within their scope.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Create a trip for a bus and route. Drivers and admins can create trips.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return one trip detail including latest location, route stops, and event summary.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Derived data: Trip detail aggregates transport views such as vw_trip_details, vw_route_stops, and vw_latest_trip_location.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Mark a trip as ended.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List student events recorded for a trip.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Create one trip student event. stopId is required for boarded and dropped_off events, and must be omitted for absent.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Note: Trip student event validation is trip-date aware: the student must have a transport assignment covering the trip date and route.

Side effects: Dropped-off events trigger parent notifications through the internal automation service.

example.message: Create Trip Student Event completed successfully

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Record one location point for a started trip.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Mark a scheduled trip as started.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Side effects: Trip start triggers parent notifications per assigned student through the internal automation service.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return the full student roster for one trip, including assigned stop coordinates, the latest event state inside the same trip, and approved home location when available.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Note: The roster returns all students assigned to the trip route for the trip date, even when no trip event has been recorded yet.

Note: If the trip exists but has no eligible students, the response remains 200 with students=[].

Note: Only approved homeLocation data is exposed to the driver-facing roster.

Derived data: Roster rows are derived from trip-date transport assignments, route stop coordinates, approved student_transport_home_locations, and the latest trip_student_events row per student inside the same trip.

example.message: Get Trip Student Roster completed successfully

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Filter roster rows by student full name or academic number.

parameters.name: stopId
parameters.description: Filter roster rows by assigned stop id.

post.description:
Create or reuse the operational trip for one route assignment, trip date, and trip type without creating duplicates.

Allowed roles: admin, driver.

Note: If a matching trip already exists for the same bus, route, tripDate, and tripType, the response remains 200 with created=false.

Note: This is the preferred driver-facing daily trip flow. POST /transport/trips remains a legacy fallback.

Derived data: The endpoint reuses the natural uniqueness of bus + route + tripDate + tripType.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List all announcements, including expired ones, for admin management.

Allowed roles: admin.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Create an admin announcement targeted to all users, one role, or multiple roles. targetRole remains legacy; targetRoles[] is the new multi-role contract.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: Announcements support roles/all only. Person-targeted announcements are not supported.

Note: Do not send targetRole and targetRoles together.

Note: If neither targetRole nor targetRoles is provided, the announcement targets all active roles.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
Return the active announcement feed visible to the authenticated role.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

Derived data: The feed uses SQL views such as vw_active_announcements and filters by targetRoles[] when present; an empty targetRoles array means all roles.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Send a direct message to another active user.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

patch.description:
Mark one received message as read.

Allowed roles: admin, parent, teacher, supervisor, driver.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

post.description:
Create admin-only multi-target direct messages as individual one-to-one copies. The endpoint is all-or-nothing and uses the same audience rules as /communication/recipients.

Allowed roles: admin.

Note: [NEW] Direct multi-target delivery creates individual copies inside messages; it does not create a group thread.

Note: [NEW] At least one of receiverUserIds[] or targetRoles[] is required.

Note: [NEW] Explicit self-targeting is rejected.

Note: [NEW] If resolved audience is empty or explicit user ids are outside the available-recipient surface, the request fails with 400.

Derived data: Audience resolution reuses the same active-user and self-exclusion policy exposed by GET /communication/recipients.

errors.message: You do not have permission to access this resource

get.description:
List a direct conversation thread with another user. The default sort is ascending by sentAt.
